From :

COMMISSION 
Roger Brown ~<E) 
~bpdt Letter from Jeremiah Hallisey Regarding Gene Rhodes 
The attached letter was received by the Commission 
after Gene Rhodes was sued by local residents to compel 
his filing of the required r-;tatement of economic interests. 
In addition, Mr. Rhodes has received a mailgram from the 
Conflicts Division notifying him that his annual statement 
was due April I, 1978, and several 
tters from the 
Fremont City Manager's office notifying him of his 
obligation to file by April 1, 1978. 
This matter is of some concern to the Enforcement 
Division because we have been investigating a formal 
complaint against Mr. Rhodes since March 13, 1978, which 
alleged certain conflicts of interest and failure to dis-
close various economic interests. 
It is important that this matter be resolved as 
expeditiously as possible. 
RB:nc 
Attachment

Memorandum 
To

Subject: 
opinion Request Meeting Participants 
FAIR POLITICAL Pa::tS COMMISSION 
Roger Brown ~ <:t:> 
Dote 
Letter from Jeremiah Hallisey Regarding Gene Rhodes 
May 5, 1978 
The attached letter was received by the Commission 
after Gene Rhodes was sued by local residents to compel 
his filing of the required statement of economic interests. 
In addition, Mr. Rhodes has received a mailgram from the 
Conflicts Division notifying him that his annucl statement 
was due April 1, 1978, and several letters from the 
Fremont City Manager's office notifying him of his 
obligation to file by April 1, 1978. 
This matter is of some concern to the Enforcement 
Division because we have been investigating a formal 
complaint against Mr. Rhodes since March 13, 1978, which 
alleged certain conflicts of interest and failure to dis-
close various economic interests. 
It is important that this matter be resolved as 
expeditiously as possible. 
RB:nc 
Attachment 


CHARLE:S A, O'BRIEN 
.JE:REMIAH /,", HALLISEY 
CHARLENE HAUGHT .JOHNSON 
STANLEY 5, TAYLOR,IIT 
ROBERT !"ATRICK MURPHY 
DANIEL 8, LERAUL 
LAW OFFICES 
O'BRIEN AND HALLISEY::' 
ONE CALIF"ORNIA STREET 
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 
May 2, 1978 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
1100 "K" Street 
Sacramento, California 
95814 
Dear Members of the Commission: 
415 433-5300 
This law firm represents l-1ayor Gene Rhodes of Fremont. 
The purpose of this letter is to request a formal opinion, or 
in the alternative, written advice, pursuant to Government Code, 
§83ll4 and 2 Cal. Adm. Code, S18320 et seq., concerning the 
duties and responsibilities of Gene Rhodes, Mayor of Fremont 
with respect to the filing of statements of economic interest 
pursuant to Government Code, S87200, et seq. 
The material facts are as follows: 
Mr. Rhodes was, 
at all times during calendar year 1977, a member of the City 
Council of the City of Fremont. 
On or about March 30, 1977, he 
filed, in a timely fashion, FPPC Form 721 covering the period 
from March 9, 1976 to December 31, 1976. 
On or about December 
29, 1977, along with his declaration of candidacy for the office 
of Mayor of the City of Fremont, he filed FPPC Form 720 disclos-
ing certain investments and interests in real property as 
required by Government Code, §8720l. 
Prior to this election, the offiae of Mayor of The 
City of Fremont had been filled by vote of the City Council from 


Fair Political Practices Commission 
May 2, 1978 
Page Two 
among its members. 
Mr. Rhodes held that position for the years 
1975 through 1977. 
By way of initiative action, the Office of 
Mayor was made a distinct elective office, and a special elec-
tion was held accordingly. 
Pursuant to this revision, the 
newly elected mayor would continue to hold a voting position on 
the City Council. 
The mayoralty election took place on March 7, 1978, 
and Mr. Rhodes emerged the winner. 
He had previously stated 
~hat regardless of the outcome of the election, he would imme-
diately resign from the City Council in order that a successor 
might also be chosen by special election. 
On March 8, he so 
resigned effective that date. 
On March 14, 1978, he was sworn 
in as Mayor. 
The basic question posed by these facts is this: 
Is 
Mayor Rhodes required to comply wi th t.he provisions of Government 
Code, §87204 with respect to his resignation from the City 
Council, and Government Code, §87202 with respect to his assump-
tion of the Office of Mayor? 
Or may he satisfy the filing 
requirements of the Political Reform Act by complying with the 
provisions of Government Code, §87203 in filing an annual state-
ment for calendar year 1977? 
Government Code, §87205 provides: 
A person who completes a term of office 
specified in §87200 and on the same day begins 
a term of the same office or another such 


Fair Political Practices Commission 
May 2, 1978 
Page Three 
office in the same jurisdiction is not deemed 
to aSsume office or leave office. 
The Commission has provided by Regulation, 2 Cal. 
Adm. Code, S18723, that: 
If a person assumes an office specified in 
Government Code §87200 between October 1 and 
December 31 and files a Statement of Economic 
Interests pursuant to Government Code §87202, 
that person need not file a Statement of 
Economic Interests pursuant to §87203 until 
one year later than the date specified in 
subsection (b). 
This regulation further provides that in certain circumstances, 
a leaving office statement may be filed in lieu of an annual 
statement. 
While neither of these provisions appears to directly 
control the instant situation, read in conjunction they evidence 
an intent to avoid unnecessary and/or repetitive filing of 
documents which would disclose the same or much the same infor-
mation. 
See also, 2 Cal. Adm. Code §18732 concerning filing 
requirements of designated employees under Conflict of Interest 
Codes. 
At all times relevant to this matter, the "jurisdiction" 
with respect to Mr. Rhodes' filing obligations was the City of 
Fremont. 
Thus, if it is the Commission's interpretation that 
Mr. Rhodes neither left nor assumed office as Mayor under these 
circumstances, it would appear that he may meet the requirements 
of the cited provisions of the Government Code by filing an 
annual statement (Form 721) covering the period of January 1, 


Fair Political Practices Commission 
May 2, 1978 
Page Four 
1977 to December 31, 1977. 
Pending determination of this matter, Mr. Rhodes is, 
with all deliberate speed, compiling the documents and informa-
tion necessary in order that the appropriate filings may be 
made. 
Resolution of this question is, however, necessary to 
enable him to comply with the filing requirements at the earliest 
possible date. 
If you have any questions, please contact me. 
Sincerely, 
Jeremiah F. Hallisey 
JFH/ci 
cc: 
The Honorable Robert Nelson 
Assistant City Manager 
Civic Center 
Fremont, California 


1

sections.question: Is Mayor Rhodes required to comply with the provisions of Government Code, §87204 with respect to his resignation from the City Council, and Government Code, §87202 with respect to his assumption of the Office of Mayor? Or may he satisfy the filing requirements of the Political Reform Act by complying with the provisions of Government Code, §87203 in filing an annual statement for calendar year 1977?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether Gene Rhodes, upon leaving the Fremont City Council to assume the newly-elected office of Mayor, must file statements of economic interest under §87204 (leaving office) and §87202 (assuming office) separately, or whether he can satisfy filing requirements through a single annual statement under §87203 for calendar year 1977.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a law firm, not an FPPC response. The document includes a memorandum from FPPC staff (Roger Brown) summarizing the matter for opinion request meeting participants. The quality of the source document is poor with significant OCR degradation, but the main request letter portion is legible. No FPPC conclusion/advice is present in this document.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Is Mayor Rhodes required to comply with the provisions of Government Code, §87204 with respect to his resignation from the City Council, and Government Code, §87202 with respect to his assumption of the Office of Mayor? Or may he satisfy the filing requirements of the Political Reform Act by complying with the provisions of Government Code, §87203 in filing an annual statement for calendar year 1977?

embedding.summary: A law firm representing Mayor Gene Rhodes of Fremont requests FPPC guidance on whether Rhodes must file separate leaving-office and assuming-office statements after resigning from the City Council to become Mayor in March 1978, or whether a single annual statement for 1977 satisfies the Political Reform Act requirements.
